warnings.description: MET-VD-007 (FEAUM 3-Year CAGR) has 5 firms with values expressed as decimals instead of percentages. FIRM-010 (EQT): 0.096 should be 9.6%, FIRM-012 (STEP): 0.264 should be 26.4%, FIRM-013 (CVC): 0.138 should be 13.8%, FIRM-015 (BLUE OWL): 0.184 should be 18.4%, FIRM-016 (MAN): 0.127 should be 12.7%. All from tier2 collection. Remaining 17 firms use percentage convention (e.g., 9.2, 17.9, 26.7). This will distort correlation analysis in step 3 if not normalized.
warnings.remediation: Standardize all MET-VD-007 values to percentage convention before step 3. Multiply decimal values by 100.

warnings.description: quantitative_tier2.json contains two entries for FIRM-009 (TPG) MET-VD-007 with conflicting values: 0.88 and 0.093. The merged file correctly resolved to 9.3% (the 0.093 decimal * 100). The 0.88 value appears to be a calculation error in the first attempt.
warnings.remediation: No action needed — merge resolved correctly. Noted for provenance.

warnings.description: quantitative_tier3.json contains two entries for FIRM-017 (BPT) MET-VD-007 with values 14.8% and 16.5%. The merged file uses 16.5%. Both derivations are documented but arrive at different results due to base year interpretation.
warnings.remediation: No action needed — merge resolved. The 16.5% value (FY2021->FY2024 base) is consistent with the 3-year CAGR convention used across other firms.

warnings.description: Three firms use proxy metrics for DE/share (MET-VD-001) with appropriate low/medium confidence flags: EQT (FIRM-010) uses ANI proxy at $0.56, confidence=low; Man Group (FIRM-011) uses Adjusted Diluted EPS at $0.323, confidence=medium; Partners Group (FIRM-014) uses adjusted IFRS EPS at $81.6/share (CHF converted), confidence=low. All properly documented with comparability caveats.
warnings.remediation: No action needed — proxy flags and confidence levels are appropriate.

warnings.description: Non-USD firms have FX conversions documented: PGHN (CHF/USD 1.11), EQT (SEK/USD 0.092), BPT (GBP/USD 1.2520 eoy / 1.2813 avg), ONEX (CAD/USD 0.731 avg), VINP (BRL/USD 0.168 eoy / 0.1863 avg), RF/Eurazeo (EUR/USD 1.08). Tier3 metadata includes comprehensive FX rate table with period-end and period-average rates for FY2020-FY2024. Rate type (period-end vs period-average) is documented per data point.
warnings.remediation: No action needed — FX documentation meets requirements.

audit_notes: CP-1 audit sampled 42 data points across 15 firms and 18 metrics. Key findings: (1) No PS-VD-9xx consulting sources cited in any quantitative data file — Dimension 3 clean. (2) No logical violations found: no negative FEAUM, no FRE margin >100%, no negative P/FRE. (3) All 78 null values have documented missing_reason fields. (4) Firm IDs correctly matched to companies across all 24 firms. (5) One HIGH-severity warning: MET-VD-007 unit inconsistency (5 firms in decimal, rest in percentage) must be fixed before step 3 standardization. This is a data quality issue, not a fabrication issue, so it does not trigger a BLOCK. The metric-architect in step 3 should normalize these during standardization. (6) 160 carry-forward data points from base run use 'source' field instead of 'source_id' — this is a schema format difference between base run and new collection, not a data quality issue. Verdict: PASSED with remediation required for W-001 before step 3.

dimension_summary.D1_invalid_premises: CLEAN — No impossible values, no firm ID mismatches, no fabricated data detected in 42 sampled claims
dimension_summary.D2_misleading_context: CLEAN with warnings — Proxy metrics (EQT ANI, MAN Adjusted EPS, PGHN adjusted IFRS EPS) properly flagged with low/medium confidence. FX conversions documented. Unit inconsistency in MET-VD-007 (W-001) is the primary concern.
dimension_summary.D3_consulting_source: CLEAN — Zero PS-VD-9xx citations in quantitative_data.json, quantitative_tier1.json, quantitative_tier2.json, or quantitative_tier3.json
dimension_summary.D4_coverage_accuracy: CLEAN — All 78 null values have missing_reason (not_disclosed, not_applicable). Reasons are plausible (e.g., BX/KKR/APO do not report constant currency growth; BPT does not report DE/share; ONEX does not report DE/share due to hybrid balance-sheet model).
